description_long: This object is a traditional Japanese torii gate, commonly found at the entrance to Shinto shrines. It features two robust cylindrical red pillars supporting a pair of horizontal crossbeams, with the uppermost beam extending outward and curving slightly upward at the ends. The structure is capped with a prominent, dark-colored roof-like element, giving it a distinctive silhouette. The base of each pillar is set in a light gray stone footing, suggesting stability and permanence. The red paint on the pillars appears weathered, adding to the sense of historical significance. The overall design is simple yet elegant, emphasizing both spiritual symbolism and architectural heritage.
description: A traditional Japanese torii gate with red cylindrical pillars, horizontal crossbeams, and a dark, curved roof-like top, set on stone bases.
description_view_0: Front view showing both red pillars, horizontal crossbeams, and the full width of the dark, curved top.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and overhang of the dark roof-like top, with one pillar visible.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying both pillars and the horizontal beams from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the roof's depth and a single pillar.
